What They Make: Application-specific specialty dyes and pigments custom-formulated for cosmetics, food, NDT (non-destructive testing), and medical uses - most under client NDA, not commodity dyes
Revenue Band: Unknown (likely <Rs.30Cr - needs verification; 100-200 employees, paid-up capital only Rs.63.8L per MCA)
Decision Maker: Gaurang Shah, Samir Shah, Sandeep Shah
DM Background: 3rd-generation family directors; described as 'techno-commercial personnel'; no specific technical degree confirmed in public sources
E1 Evidence: Own manufacturing facility in Ahmedabad since 1923; ISO 9001/14001/OHSAS 18001 certified; in-house R&D program per company site
E2 Evidence: HQ and manufacturing both in Ahmedabad (MCA-registered, ROC Ahmedabad)
C3 Evidence: Positions itself as a 'colour solution provider not a dye supplier' - custom molecule development for niche applications (NDT, medicine) under NDA. No patents, USFDA, or DSIR recognition found in public search.
C4 Evidence: Family-run, 3rd-gen directors (Shah family) with decades of combined tenure (Samir since 1995, Sandeep since 1996 per MCA). No confirmed technical/scientific pedigree found.
C5 Evidence: Dyes/specialty pigments have steady but not high-growth demand; Government Recognized Export House status signals some export tailwind.
C6 Evidence: No hiring, facility expansion, or new certification news found in the last 2 years via search. Website has no active news/press section.
C7 Evidence: Formal in-house R&D department + three ISO certifications signal some process discipline, but no ERP/SAP or MIS evidence found - needs direct verification (LinkedIn IT roles, careers page).
C8 Evidence: Three family directors of similar generation; no clearly distinct gen-2 successor with outside education/career found in public sources.
Verdict Reasoning: Genuine niche differentiation (102-year legacy, NDA-based custom dye chemistry, real R&D infrastructure) but revenue, ERP/systems use, and growth signals could not be confirmed from public web sources alone. This is a realistic 'borderline' case - promising enough to investigate further (LinkedIn job postings, direct outreach to confirm revenue band and SAP/ERP usage) rather than an outright pass or fail.
Personalization Hook: A 102-year-old Ahmedabad dye house that calls itself a 'colour solution provider, not a dye supplier' - custom-formulating dyes for aerospace NDT and medical-device applications under client NDA.
Evidence Sources: devarsons.com; LinkedIn company page; thecompanycheck.com (MCA filing data); indiamart.com company profile

Segment: Specialty/performance chemicals - oleochemicals & nutraceuticals - Basket A
What They Make: Dimer Acid, Linoleic Acid, Mixed Tocopherol Concentrate (natural Vitamin-E), Sterols - manufactured from vegetable-oil refining waste using proprietary purification process
DM Background: 30+ years in the chemical industry; B.Com from Gujarat University (Screener/company site) - not a technical/scientist founder
E1 Evidence: Own manufacturing facility at Sanand, Ahmedabad since 1995; imported German (UIC) and Swiss (Sulzer) processing equipment
E2 Evidence: HQ and manufacturing plant both in Ahmedabad district (Sanand)
C3 Evidence: India's only manufacturer of Dimer Acid; proprietary process converting vegetable-oil refining waste into high-value oleochemicals; leadership position with real barriers to entry (waste-stream sourcing + processing know-how)
C4 Evidence: Jariwala is a long-tenured industry operator (30+ yrs) but a commerce graduate, not a scientist - falls short of the 'operator built ERP/formal systems' bar without further evidence
C5 Evidence: Nutraceutical and specialty oleochemical exports have moderate global tailwind; not a top-tier policy-supported sector
C6 Evidence: 5-year sales growth only 11.9% (Screener); Q3 FY25 standalone net sales down 23.3% YoY (Moneycontrol via Tracxn) - declining, not growing
C7 Evidence: Public company with formal board/audit structure and quarterly disclosures, but no specific ERP/SAP confirmation found
C8 Evidence: Not scored - disqualified on ownership structure
Verdict Reasoning: Genuinely strong technical differentiation (India's only Dimer Acid producer) but Fairfax India Holdings - the investment vehicle of billionaire Prem Watsa - holds the controlling promoter stake (~63% per Screener; historically ~48-52% per other sources, with total promoter group ~74%). Decision-making sits with an institutional investor, not an independent founder building in-house capability - the same disqualifying pattern as the Suven Pharma example in the assignment brief. Also currently showing declining, not growing, sales.
Evidence Sources: screener.in/company/FAIRCHEMOR; fairchem.in/about-us; uncommonprofits.in (Fairchem ownership breakdown); tracxn.com (Q3 FY25 sales decline)

What They Make: CPC Blue Crude and phthalocyanine (Alpha/Beta Blue) pigments - world's largest producer of CPC blue crude with strong backward integration; expanding into AZO pigments and APIs
Decision Maker: Paru M. Jaykrishna (Chairperson); Gokul & Munjal Jaykrishna (Joint MDs)
DM Background: Family promoters; Gokul and Munjal Jaykrishna both hold Finance/Economics degrees from Lehigh University, USA and have 20+ years each in the business
E1 Evidence: Manufacturing facilities at Padra and Dahej (Vadodara district), HQ in Ahmedabad
E2 Evidence: HQ (Asahi House) is in Ahmedabad; promoter leadership based in Ahmedabad
C3 Evidence: World's largest producer of CPC Blue Crude with strong backward integration - a genuine global-scale technical moat, not a commodity position
C4 Evidence: Two joint MDs with US finance/economics degrees (Lehigh University) actively running operations for 20+ years each - clear operator-founder profile
C5 Evidence: Pigments for paints/inks/plastics/textiles is a stable, export-oriented sector; company diversifying into APIs for additional tailwind
Verdict Reasoning: Revenue Rs.535Cr (FY26) exceeds the Rs.500Cr ceiling - would likely have scored A-band on nearly every other criterion (global-scale technical differentiation, Lehigh-educated operator-founders, genuine promoter control at ~67% holding). Worth flagging: check whether the company reports segment-wise or subsidiary-level revenue that might isolate a sub-Rs.500Cr specialty unit, since 'check current revenue, not just last annual report' cuts both ways in a fast-growing sector.

Segment: Specialty/performance chemicals - chlor-alkali derivatives - Basket A
What They Make: Chloromethanes (MDC, Chloroform, CTC), CPVC Resin, Epichlorohydrin, Hydrogen Peroxide, Caustic Soda/Potash - India's largest CPVC resin plant and first Epichlorohydrin plant
Revenue Band: Rs.500Cr+ (Rs.2550Cr total income FY25 per Screener/Allvest.co)
DM Background: Promoter-founder from the Meghmani group family; company demerged from Meghmani Organics in 2021
E1 Evidence: Fully integrated automated manufacturing complex at Dahej (PCPIR region) with captive power plants
E2 Evidence: HQ in Ahmedabad; primary manufacturing at Dahej within Gujarat
C3 Evidence: 1st company in India to set up an Epichlorohydrin plant; largest-capacity CPVC resin plant in India; backward-and-forward integrated complex - genuine capability-based differentiation
C5 Evidence: CPVC/specialty derivatives directly benefit from Make-in-India import substitution (CPVC and Epichlorohydrin were previously fully imported)
Verdict: Fail - Auto-disqualify (revenue far exceeds Rs.500Cr ceiling)
Verdict Reasoning: Revenue has scaled to ~Rs.2550Cr total income (FY25) - far past the Rs.500Cr ceiling; market cap ~Rs.7900Cr. This is a useful pattern to flag in the methodology doc: several of Ahmedabad's most 'differentiated' specialty chemical companies have already scaled past DT's target band, meaning genuine Federer-fit candidates in this city are more likely to be found among unlisted, earlier-stage promoter-driven firms than among the well-known listed names that show up first in search.

Segment: Specialty food & nutraceutical ingredients - botanical extracts - Basket B
What They Make: Standardized herbal/botanical extracts (ashwagandha, green tea, aloe vera, amla, and 25+ others) for nutraceuticals, herbal biomedicines, food supplements and cosmetics
Revenue Band: <Rs.30Cr (~USD 3M / ~Rs.25Cr per RocketReach; ~5 employees per RocketReach vs 51-200 range elsewhere - likely on the smaller end)
DM Background: Not found - no named founder/MD in public search results
E1 Evidence: Own processing facility in Ahmedabad with procurement, processing, QC, R&D, and packaging departments per company site; GMP/Kosher/Halal certified
E2 Evidence: Registered office and operations in Ahmedabad (Makarba)
C3 Evidence: GMP/Kosher/Halal-certified botanical extracts, but botanical extraction is a crowded field (118+ active competitors per Tracxn) with no patents, DSIR recognition, or proprietary process claimed
C4 Evidence: No identifiable technical or scientist founder found; company structure (originally a sole proprietorship, incorporated as Pvt Ltd only in 2019) and ~5-employee headcount suggest thin leadership bench
C5 Evidence: Botanical extracts / nutraceutical ingredients have genuine global export tailwind (WHO-cited rising demand for herbal medicine)
C6 Evidence: No hiring, expansion, or new-certification news found in the last 2 years; website has no active press/news section
C7 Evidence: No ERP, MIS, or digital infrastructure evidence found; very small team size makes structured systems unlikely
C8 Evidence: No succession or professional-management evidence found
Verdict: Fail - Not ICP (too small, thin infrastructure)
Verdict Reasoning: Looks like a segment fit on paper (botanical extracts, Basket B) but fails on scale and systems: revenue is well under Rs.30Cr, headcount is tiny, and no decision-maker, ERP, or growth signal could be confirmed anywhere in public sources. This is a useful 'looks-right-but-isn't' example: segment and city match, but there is no real operational infrastructure yet for DT to build on.

Segment: Complex/regulated pharma - Novel Drug Delivery Systems (NDDS) - Basket B
What They Make: Patented pain-management injectables (Dynapar AQ - world's first painless diclofenac injection, Dynapar QPS, NASO B12 nasal Vitamin-B12 delivery); WHO-GMP facilities exporting to 85+ countries
Revenue Band: Rs.500Cr+ (Rs.967Cr FY24, up from Rs.843Cr FY23)
DM Background: Gold medalist B.Pharm/M.Pharm, L.M. College of Pharmacy; PhD in Pharmaceutical Technology; holds multiple patents including a US patent - genuine scientist-founder
E1 Evidence: Three WHO-GMP facilities (Thol and Sanand, Gujarat; Dehradun) with defined production capacity per CARE Ratings report
E2 Evidence: HQ (Troikaa House) in Ahmedabad; two of three facilities in Gujarat (Thol, Sanand)
C3 Evidence: Multiple NDDS patents; Dynapar AQ is world's first painless diclofenac injection; National R&D Award 2015 from India's Ministry of Science and Technology
C4 Evidence: PhD pharmacist-scientist founder with 40+ years and personal patent portfolio; gen-2 (Asheel, Nisheel Patel) already in marketing/R&D leadership per CARE Ratings
C5 Evidence: Complex/regulated NDDS pharma has steady but not strongly policy-boosted tailwind
C6 Evidence: Revenue grew ~15% YoY (Rs.843Cr to Rs.967Cr, FY23-FY24); new patent launches (Xykaa IM, 2023) and active global trade-show presence (CPHI Frankfurt 2025)
C7 Evidence: WHO-GMP certification across 3 facilities implies real quality systems, but no direct ERP/SAP confirmation found
C8 Evidence: Gen-2 (Asheel Ketan Patel - US-educated, Nisheel Ketan Patel) in operational roles; Joint MD Milan R. Patel (Chemical Engineer, 35+ yrs) per CARE Ratings
Verdict Reasoning: Excellent Federer profile on almost every criterion - PhD scientist-founder, real patents, multi-facility WHO-GMP systems, clear gen-2 succession already active - but FY24 revenue of Rs.967Cr is well past the Rs.500Cr ceiling. Correction note: an earlier unverified draft of this research had listed Troikaa's revenue as Rs.100-300Cr and scored it as a top pass (76/100) - that figure was wrong; always verify current revenue directly rather than trusting an unsourced number.
Personalization Hook: Invented the world's first painless diclofenac injection and holds patents in 90+ countries for it - but has scaled past DT's target revenue band.

Segment: Complex/regulated pharma - sterile injectables - Basket B
What They Make: Generic and specialty sterile injectables (liquid vials, lyo vials, ampoules, pre-filled syringes, eye drops) exported to 80+ countries; EU-GMP, Australian TGA, Brazilian ANVISA, Mexican Cofepris approved facilities
Revenue Band: Rs.100-500Cr (Tracxn: Rs.100-500Cr band, 17% 1-yr revenue CAGR, 14% EBITDA CAGR - genuinely growing)
DM Title: Director (30% long-term equity partner, in charge of day-to-day operations per India Ratings press release)
DM Background: Correcting a name error from an earlier unverified draft that called him 'Naresh Shah' - his correct name is Naishadh Vikrambhai Shah, export-and-operations-focused promoter
E1 Evidence: Two manufacturing units in Gujarat with 50+ global regulatory agency accreditations (EU-GMP, TGA, ANVISA, Cofepris, PIC/s) per India Ratings
E2 Evidence: HQ in Ahmedabad (Bodakdev/Thaltej); both manufacturing units in Gujarat
C3 Evidence: 1,000+ approved dossiers across ~190 molecules; 15-member in-house R&D team with sterile development capabilities including liposomal, microsphere, and depot injections - genuine technical depth
C4 Evidence: Not scored - disqualified on ownership structure
C5 Evidence: Sterile injectables for global regulated markets is a strong-growth, export-oriented pharma sub-sector
C6 Evidence: 17% revenue CAGR and 14% EBITDA CAGR (Tracxn); actively hiring (Regulatory Affairs Manager, per LinkedIn); consistent exhibitor at CPHI Frankfurt/India
C7 Evidence: Global regulatory accreditation implies real quality-systems discipline; no direct ERP/SAP confirmation found
C8 Evidence: Not scored - disqualified on ownership structure
Verdict: Fail - Auto-disqualify (majority acquired by Eris Lifesciences in Feb 2024)
Verdict Reasoning: IMPORTANT CORRECTION: an earlier unverified draft scored this company 56/100 ('Investigate/Potential') without catching a critical fact - in February 2024, listed pharma company Eris Lifesciences acquired a 51% controlling stake in Swiss Parenterals (with the Eris promoter group acquiring a further 19%, for 70% combined institutional/strategic control), per an India Ratings press release. Naishadh Shah retains only a 30% stake as a 'long-term equity partner.' This is the same disqualifying pattern as Fairchem Organics elsewhere in this list - genuine technical strength, but decision-making now sits with an acquiring public company, not an independent founder. Good example of why M&A/growth-signal searches must be run for every candidate.
Evidence Sources: swiss.in/about-swiss; tracxn.com; indiaratings.co.in/pressrelease/68566 (Eris acquisition - critical finding); zaubacorp.com/SWISS-PARENTERALS-LIMITED

Segment: Contract Research Organization (CRO) - pharma/chemical testing services - services adjacent to Basket A/B
What They Make: Regulatory testing, analytical development, biocompatibility, preclinical/toxicological, and stability testing services for pharmaceutical, medical device, chemical, and food companies; DSIR-approved in-house R&D centre
Revenue Band: Rs.30-100Cr (Tofler: Rs.1-100Cr operating revenue band FY23; RocketReach ~Rs.50Cr, 192 employees - roughly consistent)
DM Background: M.Pharm, PhD, plus multiple additional professional/legal qualifications (PGDIBM, LLB, FRSB, DABT); genuine scientist-founder with deep toxicology/regulatory expertise
E1 Evidence: 85,000 sq.ft. testing facility on a 5-acre campus with a further 35,000 sq.ft. expansion underway; DSIR-approved in-house R&D centre - NOTE: this is a services/testing company, not a physical-goods manufacturer, so it should be flagged for direct discussion of whether DT's E1 'Producer' gate includes CROs (a common false-positive trap per the assignment's own examples)
E2 Evidence: HQ and full facility located in Ahmedabad (Matoda, Changodar-Bawla Highway)
C3 Evidence: 9+ international accreditations (ISO/IEC 17025, OECD-GLP, AAALAC, USFDA, EMA, ASCA-A2LA); DSIR-recognised R&D centre; 11 patented polyherbal formulations developed in-house - genuine, verifiable technical depth rare among CROs this size
C4 Evidence: CEO holds a PhD and multiple specialist certifications (toxicology, risk assessment); co-directors Dr. Rina Gokani (PhD) and Mayur Kandoriya (MBA) form a credentialed, complementary leadership team
C5 Evidence: Regulatory testing/CRO services for pharma and med-device sectors is a structurally growing segment as India's pharma exports and medical-device manufacturing scale up
C6 Evidence: Facility expansion (additional 35,000 sq.ft.) explicitly described as 'in final stage' per company site; continued CPHI Frankfurt/global trade-show presence in 2025 - genuine recent growth signal
C7 Evidence: No ERP/SAP/MIS evidence found; very low authorized/paid-up share capital (Rs.1 lakh) suggests a lean, possibly still process-light back office despite technical sophistication
C8 Evidence: Three-director structure (Rachchh, Gokani, Kandoriya) with complementary but same-generation backgrounds; no distinct next-gen successor identified
Verdict: Investigate - Strong candidate contingent on services-vs-manufacturer gate interpretation
Verdict Reasoning: Genuinely exceptional technical and leadership profile (DSIR R&D recognition, PhD-led team, global accreditations) and a real growth signal (facility expansion). The one open question is structural: Accuprec is a testing/research services company, not a physical-product manufacturer, and the assignment materials specifically flag CRO-type companies as a common trap for the E1 'Producer' gate. Recommend a direct question to DT/Internshala about whether service-based technical businesses qualify, rather than assuming either way.
Personalization Hook: A DSIR-recognized, PhD-led CRO that developed 11 of its own patented polyherbal formulations in-house - unusually deep IP generation for a testing-services company its size.
Evidence Sources: accuprec.com; crunchbase.com/organization/accuprec-research-labs; zaubacorp.com; tofler.in/accuprec-research-labs; cphi-online.com company listing

Segment: Complex/regulated pharma - sterile parenterals - Basket B
What They Make: Sterile IV injectables and IV fluids in glass and PP bottles via Blow-Fill-Seal (BFS) technology; antibiotic, diuretic, anti-anaerobic injections
Revenue Band: Rs.30-100Cr (net sales ~Rs.41Cr per MarketsMojo FY25 standalone figure; other aggregators show ~Rs.176Cr, likely a different consolidation basis - needs direct confirmation)
DM Background: Long-tenured promoter family; correcting an error from an earlier unverified draft that named Dinesh B. Patel as MD - Dinesh Patel is actually Chairman & Non-Executive Director, not MD
E1 Evidence: Manufacturing facility at Chhatral, Kalol (Gandhinagar dist.) since 1980; WHO-GMP/ISO certified; BFS plant since 1995/2007
E2 Evidence: Corporate office in Ahmedabad (Satellite); factory in Kalol, Gandhinagar dist. - within the wider Ahmedabad operational corridor but not Ahmedabad city itself, worth flagging per the assignment's city-definition guidance
C3 Evidence: BFS technology for IV fluids is a real capability edge over basic glass-bottle manufacturers, but sterile IV fluids is a fairly crowded regulated-generics category with no patents or DSIR recognition found
C4 Evidence: Promoter-family-run (3 generations of Patels on the board per Zaubacorp); no confirmed scientist/PhD background, but 40+ year single-industry tenure and repeated capacity expansions (2018, 2021) suggest real engagement
C5 Evidence: Sterile IV fluids/parenterals has stable domestic hospital demand and some export tailwind, no strong policy-driven boost identified
C6 Evidence: Tracxn shows only ~4% 1-year revenue CAGR and a -18% EBITDA CAGR (deteriorating profitability); most recent capacity expansion completed back in June 2021, outside the assignment's 18-month growth-signal window
C7 Evidence: WHO-GMP certification implies documented quality systems, but no direct ERP/SAP/MIS evidence found
C8 Evidence: Multiple family directors of a similar generation (Himanshu, Anar, Dinesh Patel per MCA filings) but no clearly distinct, differently-educated gen-2 successor identified
Verdict Reasoning: Real BFS manufacturing capability and 40+ year operating history, but financials show declining EBITDA and flat revenue growth (not the 'actively growing' profile DT wants), and no clear succession or systems evidence beyond WHO-GMP certification. Correction note: an earlier unverified draft misnamed the Managing Director as Dinesh B. Patel instead of Himanshu C. Patel - always verify leadership names directly against MCA/company filings before using them in outreach.
Personalization Hook: Manufactures sterile IV fluids using Blow-Fill-Seal technology - a more advanced process than the standard glass-bottle IV manufacturing still common among smaller competitors.

Segment: Performance chemicals - food/dye colors - Basket A
What They Make: Food colors, lake colors, blended colors, FD&C colors, dye intermediates, salt-free dyes, D&C colors, natural food colors; listed on BSE/NSE
Revenue Band: Rs.300-360Cr (PitchBook: trailing 12-month revenue $42.7M as of Sep-2025, ~Rs.355Cr at current exchange rate)
DM Background: Correcting a name error from an earlier unverified draft that called him 'Bhagwandas Kalani' - his correct surname is Patel; long-tenured promoter, 468 employees per PitchBook, company established 1990
E1 Evidence: Own manufacturing facility in Ahmedabad (Sola); ISO 9001, ISO 14001, FSSC 22000, and GMP certified; USFDA-certified food color production per company site
E2 Evidence: Registered office and manufacturing both in Ahmedabad (Sola / Sarkhej-Gandhinagar Highway)
C3 Evidence: Genuine technical certification depth (FSSC 22000, USFDA-certified food colors) but food/dye coloring is a moderately commoditized category globally with several large competitors; no patents or DSIR recognition found
C4 Evidence: Family promoter structure (Bhagwandas K. Patel as Chairman/MD, sons Rameshbhai and Dixit B. Patel as Joint Managing Directors) - multi-generational family operation, no confirmed scientist/technical founder background
C5 Evidence: Food colors and dye intermediates have steady global export demand, not a high-growth policy-boosted category
C6 Evidence: Listed company (BSE: 532707, NSE: DYNPRO) with continued export operations to multiple markets; no major recent expansion/hiring news found in search
C7 Evidence: Public-company disclosure requirements imply baseline financial systems, but no direct ERP/SAP confirmation found
C8 Evidence: Two sons (Rameshbhai and Dixit B. Patel) already serving as Joint Managing Directors alongside the founder-chairman - real succession structure already in place, though same-generation, not yet a distinct third generation
Verdict: Investigate - solid core asset with commodity risk
Verdict Reasoning: Correction note: an earlier unverified draft misnamed the Chairman as 'Bhagwandas Kalani' - his correct name is Bhagwandas K. Patel. The company is real, listed, and has genuine multi-certification manufacturing depth, but sits closer to commodity dye/food-color territory than a highly differentiated specialty player, and growth signals are flat rather than accelerating - worth direct outreach to assess systems maturity and current growth plans rather than a confident pass or fail from public data alone.

City: Vapi/Vadodara (ROC-registered Ahmedabad; HQ address also listed as Mumbai in some filings)
Segment: Complex/regulated pharma - APIs & intermediates - Basket B
What They Make: Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) across anti-inflammatory, anti-hyperlipidemic, anti-depressant, anti-diabetic, anti-convulsant, anti-fungal, and vitamin categories (e.g. Diclofenac salts, Atorvastatin, Rosuvastatin, Rivaroxaban, Apixaban); Contract Research and Custom Synthesis (CRAMS) services
Revenue Band: Rs.500Cr+ (Tracxn: Rs.642Cr revenue FY22, 7% 1-yr CAGR; company itself states Mumbai HQ, not Ahmedabad)
DM Background: Correcting a name error from an earlier unverified draft that called the founder 'Umed Dosanjh' - the actual founder is Umed Doshi, and the current MD/CEO running operations is his son Manish Umed Doshi
E1 Evidence: Two WHO-GMP manufacturing sites approved by USFDA, EDQM/EU-GMP, PMDA (Japan), TGA (Australia), ANVISA (Brazil), and COFEPRIS (Mexico); 12,000 sq.ft. in-house R&D centre per CPHI listing
E2 Evidence: Registered office is technically under ROC Ahmedabad, but the company's actual operating address per Zaubacorp/CPHI/Tracxn filings is Vapi (GIDC) or Mumbai (Nariman Point HQ), not Ahmedabad city - a real accessibility/E2 concern, not just a technicality
C3 Evidence: Broad, technically sophisticated API portfolio including complex molecules (Rivaroxaban, Apixaban, Mirabegron); genuine CRAMS capability alongside standard API production; global regulatory approvals across 6 major agencies
C5 Evidence: Regulated API/CRAMS exports to global pharma majors is a strong, policy-supported growth category (China+1 sourcing shift)
Verdict: Fail - Auto-disqualify (revenue exceeds Rs.500Cr ceiling; also fails Ahmedabad-city E2 gate)
Verdict Reasoning: Double correction needed here: (1) an earlier unverified draft named the founder 'Umed Dosanjh' - the real name is Umed Doshi, with son Manish Umed Doshi now running operations as MD/CEO; (2) revenue is Rs.642Cr (FY22) - well past the Rs.500Cr ceiling, not the 'Rs.300-500Cr, B-Potential' scoring given in the earlier draft; (3) the company's real operational base appears to be Vapi/Mumbai rather than Ahmedabad city itself, despite ROC-Ahmedabad registration - a second, independent reason it likely should not have been counted as an Ahmedabad candidate at all.

Segment: Complex/regulated pharma - finished formulations (FDF) - Basket B
What They Make: Solid oral dosage forms (tablets, capsules, dry syrups, oral powders including cephalosporins), liquids, injections, and ointments; CMO/CDMO contract manufacturing for global pharma majors
Revenue Band: Rs.150-200Cr (Tracxn: Rs.196Cr FY25, 6% 1-yr revenue CAGR, 5% EBITDA CAGR)
Decision Maker: Dr. Viranchi Shah (Managing Director); Vinit Arvindbhai Shah (Chairman)

E1 Evidence: Ultramodern manufacturing facility at Chachrawadi Vasna, Sanand (Ahmedabad district) complying with international GMP norms; company describes itself as 'four decades old' (originally founded 1981, current entity incorporated 2019)
E2 Evidence: Corporate office in Ahmedabad (Prahladnagar/Gulbai Tekra); manufacturing facility in Sanand, Ahmedabad district
C3 Evidence: In-house formulation/development wing for novel delivery systems (bi-layered, mouth-dispersible, sustained-release tablets); genuine technical capability but finished-dose generics manufacturing is a fairly crowded category without unique molecule-level differentiation
C4 Evidence: MD Dr. Viranchi Shah is President of IDMA (national industry association) - a strong, verifiable signal of both technical credibility and sector-wide standing; family co-leadership (Vinit, Rushabh, Ruchir Shah on the board) suggests active multi-generational involvement
C5 Evidence: Finished-dose generic pharma exports to 40+ countries is a stable, moderately growing category, not a high-tailwind niche
C6 Evidence: Claims 30% YoY growth since inception per LinkedIn; actively hiring (Business Development Associate posting); recently added CMO/CDMO business line (started ~2017-18) as a new growth vector per company MD interview
C7 Evidence: No ERP/SAP/MIS evidence found in public sources
C8 Evidence: Multiple Shah-family directors of a similar generation (Rutvik, Rushabh, Ruchir, Viranchi, Vinit) already on the board - broad family involvement, but no clearly distinct next-generation successor with an outside background identified
Verdict: Investigate - genuinely promising, led by a recognized industry figure
Verdict Reasoning: Correction note: an earlier unverified draft misnamed the founder as 'Dr. C.S. Shah' - the real Managing Director is Dr. Viranchi Shah, who also serves as President of IDMA, a strong and independently verifiable leadership signal. Revenue (Rs.196Cr) fits comfortably within the target band, and the CMO/CDMO growth vector plus claimed 30% YoY growth make this one of the stronger candidates in this batch - worth prioritizing for direct outreach.
Personalization Hook: MD Dr. Viranchi Shah is the sitting President of IDMA (Indian Drug Manufacturers Association) - a genuinely well-connected industry leader running a four-decade-old, actively growing formulations business.

Segment: Complex/regulated pharma - sterile dosages (Blow-Fill-Seal) - Basket B
What They Make: Sterile dosage forms via Blow-Fill-Seal (BFS) technology spanning fluid therapy, ophthalmics, respiratory, irrigation solutions, and anti-infectives; contract manufactures sterile dosage products for other pharma majors
Revenue Band: Rs.500Cr+ (RocketReach/martini.ai: ~$62.3M annual revenue, roughly Rs.520Cr at current exchange rate)
DM Background: Correcting a name error from an earlier unverified draft that called the MD 'Anand Patel' - the actual Managing Director per RocketReach company records is Bhavesh Patel
E1 Evidence: Manufacturing facility in Matar, Kheda district (outside Ahmedabad city); founded 1995 as Marck Parenterals (India) Ltd before rebranding; positions itself as 'India's most versatile sterile dosages pharmaceutical company in Blow Fill Seal Space'
E2 Evidence: Corporate office is in Ahmedabad (Ashram Road), but the actual manufacturing facility is in Matar, Kheda district - a meaningful distance from Ahmedabad city, similar to the Denis Chem Lab case elsewhere in this list; worth flagging for the assignment's city-definition question
C3 Evidence: Genuine technical specialization in Blow-Fill-Seal sterile manufacturing across a wide product range (fluid therapy to ophthalmics to respiratory) - a real capability moat versus simpler glass-bottle competitors
C4 Evidence: Long-tenured promoter-MD (Bhavesh Patel) but background/credentials not confirmed in public sources beyond his role
C5 Evidence: Sterile dosage/BFS pharma has steady domestic and some export demand, no strong recent policy tailwind identified
C6 Evidence: No recent (within 18 months) hiring, expansion, or certification news found; historical credit-rating data (martini.ai, citing a CARE D rating as of FY12) flags a past period of credit/liquidity stress - worth checking whether this has since been resolved
C7 Evidence: No ERP/SAP/MIS evidence found in public sources

Verdict Reasoning: Correction note: an earlier unverified draft misnamed the MD as 'Anand Patel' (actual name: Bhavesh Patel) and estimated revenue at Rs.100-200Cr; the real figure is closer to Rs.520Cr, which places this company just past the Rs.500Cr ceiling. Genuine BFS technical specialization, but also carries a historical credit-risk flag (CARE D rating noted for FY12 in third-party credit research) that would be worth clarifying if revenue is later found to be within range on a standalone/segment basis.
Evidence Sources: rocketreach.co/marck-biosciences-ltd-profile; martini.ai (credit research, incl. historical CARE D rating); medindia.net; zoominfo.com/c/marck-biosciences-ltd

City: Vadodara (ROC-registered Ahmedabad, corporate address in Vadodara)
Segment: Complex/regulated pharma - formulations & empty gelatin capsules - Basket B
What They Make: Wide range of pharmaceutical dosage forms (tablets, capsules, syrups, injectables) plus Empty Hard Gelatin Capsules - the only Gujarat company manufacturing both formulations and empty capsules under one roof; also has a USFDA-standard plant in Himachal Pradesh
Revenue Band: Rs.100-500Cr (Tracxn: Rs.100-500Cr band, 12% 1-yr revenue CAGR, 20% EBITDA CAGR)
DM Background: Correcting a location and leadership error from an earlier unverified draft that called this an 'Ahmedabad' company led by 'Amit Patel' - the company's actual corporate office and registered address is in Vadodara (Baroda), not Ahmedabad city, and MCA filings show the board is led by Jagjeetsingh (J.) Channa alongside Satvinder Pal Kaur and others - no 'Amit Patel' found among directors
E1 Evidence: WHO and ISO-compliant plants; DSIR-approved R&D laboratory working on NDDS and biotechnology products per company LinkedIn; USFDA-standard plant added in Himachal Pradesh
E2 Evidence: Corporate office and ROC-registered address are both in Vadodara, not Ahmedabad city - this fails the city-scope requirement even though the company is Gujarat-based and has some Ahmedabad-adjacent presence
C3 Evidence: Unique in Gujarat for combining formulations manufacturing and empty hard gelatin capsule production under one roof; DSIR-approved R&D lab is a genuine differentiator, though the core formulations business is fairly standard generics
C4 Evidence: Founded in 1986 by a team of pharma professionals; current chairman J. Channa's specific background not confirmed in public sources beyond his board role
C5 Evidence: Generic formulations plus capsule manufacturing is a stable, moderately growing pharma sub-sector
C6 Evidence: 12% revenue CAGR and a strong 20% EBITDA CAGR (Tracxn) - genuinely improving profitability, not just top-line growth
C7 Evidence: DSIR-approved R&D lab and multi-state, multi-plant operations imply reasonably mature internal systems, but no direct ERP/SAP confirmation found
C8 Evidence: No clear founder-to-next-generation succession structure identified in public sources; board composition (multiple Sethi/Singh-surnamed directors) suggests a professionally managed rather than single-family structure
Verdict Reasoning: Correction note: an earlier unverified draft misidentified this as an Ahmedabad company led by 'Amit Patel.' In reality, Comed Chemicals is headquartered and ROC-registered in Vadodara, and its actual board (per MCA/Zaubacorp filings) is led by J. Channa alongside several Sethi/Singh-surnamed directors - no Amit Patel appears among them. The company is otherwise a reasonably interesting Gujarat pharma player (DSIR R&D lab, unique capsule+formulation combination, strong EBITDA growth), but does not qualify for this specific Ahmedabad-city research batch.

Segment: Specialty/performance chemicals - vinyl sulphone & custom synthesis - Basket A
What They Make: Vinyl sulphone dyes intermediates (OAVS, PCVS, DMAVS, Bronner's VS) plus performance chemicals, surfactants, and emulsifiers for agrochemicals, personal care, textiles, paints, and oil & gas; also offers custom synthesis services
Revenue Band: Rs.150-500Cr+ (RocketReach: ~$23.9M/~Rs.200Cr with 315 employees; one B2B directory listing states 'Above Rs.500 Crore Approx.' - conflicting figures, needs direct verification)
DM Background: 2nd-generation promoter; took over and diversified the business from its founder, the late Suryakant Jani, who established the company in 1993 - a genuine, verifiable succession story
E1 Evidence: Three manufacturing plots across Vatva GIDC (Phase I, II) and a Dahej facility; ISO 9001:2015, KOSHER, and Halal certified; dedicated R&D department credited by the company as central to its product success
E2 Evidence: Registered office and primary manufacturing (2 of 3 plants) located in Vatva GIDC, Ahmedabad
C3 Evidence: Genuine technical specialization in vinyl sulphone dye intermediates plus expansion into performance chemicals/custom synthesis - real process chemistry depth (sulfonation, ethoxylation, propoxylation, alkoxylation, esterification, acetylation, reduction) rather than simple trading
C4 Evidence: Clear, verifiable 2nd-generation succession: founder (late Suryakant Jani, 1993) to son/promoter Ruchit Jani, who actively diversified the company into performance chemicals starting 2009 - exactly the kind of succession-in-progress structure DT is looking for
C5 Evidence: Specialty/performance chemicals for agro, personal care, and textile end-markets have steady global demand, not an exceptional policy tailwind
C6 Evidence: Active, current social media/LinkedIn presence with regular content (Women's Day, National Science Day posts) and product-line announcements as recently as 2026 per LinkedIn activity dates - a genuine current-activity signal, though not a major expansion/hiring announcement specifically
C7 Evidence: ISO 9001:2015 certification and multi-plant operations imply baseline systems discipline, but no direct ERP/SAP confirmation found - a plausible PDGMS opportunity
C8 Evidence: Explicit, well-documented 2nd-generation transition already completed and actively driving new business lines (performance chemicals since 2009) - one of the clearest succession stories in this entire batch
Verdict: Investigate - strong succession-story candidate, revenue band needs direct confirmation
Verdict Reasoning: A genuinely promising candidate: real 2nd-generation succession already underway (exactly DT's ICP pattern), real process chemistry depth, and Ahmedabad-based manufacturing. The one open item is revenue - RocketReach suggests ~Rs.200Cr while a B2B directory listing claims 'above Rs.500Cr,' a meaningful discrepancy that needs direct verification (e.g., via Tofler/MCA filing or direct outreach) before finalizing the score, since a revenue figure above Rs.500Cr would auto-disqualify this otherwise strong candidate.
Personalization Hook: A dye-intermediate specialist where the 2nd-generation promoter, Ruchit Jani, personally led the company's expansion into an entirely new product category (performance chemicals) starting in 2009 - a clean, documented succession-driven growth story.

City: Mehsana, Gujarat (~30km from Ahmedabad, outside Ahmedabad city/district)
Segment: Performance chemicals - dyes, pigments & precipitated silica - Basket A
What They Make: Vinyl Sulphone and H-Acid (dye intermediates for reactive dyes), CPC Green pigment (printing ink/plastic/paint), and Precipitated Silica (tyre, rubber, agrochemical, animal feed applications)
Revenue Band: Rs.300-400Cr (Screener: Rs.372Cr revenue, market cap Rs.201Cr)
Decision Maker: Paru M. Jaykrishna (Chairperson & MD); Gokul & Munjal Jaykrishna (Joint MDs)
DM Background: Same promoter family as Asahi Songwon Colors (also in this batch, auto-disqualified for revenue) - Paru Jaykrishna founded this company in 1989; Munjal Jaykrishna holds a Bachelor's in Business/Economics from Lehigh University and previously worked as a Financial Analyst at Bank of California
E1 Evidence: Manufacturing plant in the Mehsana chemical belt plus a newer Precipitated Silica plant at Dahej (commissioned 2022, ramped to 18,000 MTPA by FY25); ISO 9001:2008 certified
E2 Evidence: Plant is located in Mehsana district, described by the company itself as '30 KM away from the Ahmedabad International Airport' - genuinely close to Ahmedabad but not within Ahmedabad city or district itself, a real E2 concern for this specific research batch
C3 Evidence: Diversified product base (Vinyl Sulphone, H-Acid, CPC Green pigment, Precipitated Silica) shows real technical range, but CARE Ratings' own assessment notes 'limited product diversity compared to larger, more integrated players' and 'high volatility in operating performance' - moderate rather than strong differentiation
C4 Evidence: Same well-credentialed promoter family as Asahi Songwon Colors - founder Paru Jaykrishna is a recognized industry figure (first woman VP of Gujarat Chamber of Commerce), and Joint MD Munjal Jaykrishna has a Lehigh University finance/economics degree and prior Wall Street-adjacent experience
C5 Evidence: Precipitated silica (tyre/rubber applications) and dye intermediates serve moderately growing industrial end-markets, with stricter pollution norms favoring organized players like AksharChem per CARE Ratings
C6 Evidence: CARE Ratings (Feb 2026) reports a net loss of Rs.5.24Cr in 9MFY26 versus a Rs.3.33Cr profit in the same period last year, driven by a 50% US tariff on Indian exports and an underperforming new silica plant that has not yet reached breakeven - a clear recent financial deterioration, not a growth signal
C7 Evidence: Public company with formal board, quarterly disclosures, and a comfortable capital structure (gearing of 0.25x) per CARE Ratings, but no direct ERP/SAP confirmation found
C8 Evidence: Two Joint MDs (Gokul and Munjal Jaykrishna) already running finance and operations respectively alongside founder-Chairperson Paru Jaykrishna - an active family succession structure, though same as the related Asahi Songwon Colors entity
Verdict: Fail - Not Ahmedabad city, plus recent financial deterioration
Verdict Reasoning: A related entity to Asahi Songwon Colors (same Jaykrishna promoter family) that actually falls within the Rs.50-500Cr revenue band, unlike its sister company - but its plant is in Mehsana, roughly 30km from Ahmedabad, which places it outside this specific research batch's Ahmedabad city/district scope. Independently, recent financials (a 9MFY26 net loss per CARE Ratings, driven by US tariffs and an underperforming new plant) would also count against it on the C6 growth-signal criterion even if location were not an issue - a useful example of why 'same promoter family as a strong candidate' does not automatically transfer a positive verdict.
